explanations: The suffix -tuṃ is added to the root of the verb or the verbal base with or sometimes without the connecting vowel -i- to form the infinitive.

Examples of infinitive formation: pac + i + tuṃ = pacituṃ (to cook); khād + i + tuṃ = khādituṃ (to eat); gam + tuṃ = gantuṃ (to go); dā + tuṃ = dātuṃ (to give); ṭhā + tuṃ = ṭhātuṃ (to stand); pā + tuṃ = pātuṃ / pivituṃ (to drink).

examples.english: The farmer wishes to plough the field.
The child climbs the tree to eat fruits.

examples.pali: Manussā samaṇehi pañhe pucchituṃ vihāraṃ āgacchanti.
examples.english: Men come to the monastery to ask questions from the recluses.

examples.pali: Kumārā kīḷituṃ mittehi saha samuddaṃ gacchanti.
examples.english: Boys go to the sea with friends to play.
